steps.title: Identify the present-tense signal
steps.content: The main clause “An emulsifier is a type of compound” establishes present tense to describe a general, timeless definition of what an emulsifier is—not a historical event or one-time action.

steps.title: Apply verb tense consistency
steps.content:
The rule is that a relative clause describing a general function should match the main clause's present tense. “Serves” (choice C) is simple present, which maintains consistency with the main clause's “is.” The other choices use past tense forms (served, had served, was serving), which would wrongly suggest a past action rather than a general characteristic.

That matches choice C — C is correct.
